title: Uniface Mappings to Installable Character Sets
breadcrumbs: Uniface Mappings to Installable Character Sets
text: Uniface Mappings to Installable Character Sets During Uniface installation, it is possible to choose a character set that is appropriate for one or more languages. Thus Western European is appropriate for English, German, French and so on, and Cyrillic is appropriate for Slavic languages such as Russian. The character set name is generic, for example Western European, Cyrillic, or Simplified Chinese, but the actual character set installed is specific to the platform. Different platforms use different character set standards. Uniface uses one name to represent the different character sets that represent the same characters. For example, CP1251 and ISO 8859-5 are character sets in different platforms to represent Cyrillic characters. Uniface uses Cyrillic to represent all of them. The generic character set is also referred to as a meta variant . Each meta variant is mapped to several installable character sets, as listed in the following table, in which the column headings mean the following: Type—Type of character sets. For more information, see Types of Character Sets . Meta Variant—Generic character set name used in Uniface, for example, in the Character set selection screen during Uniface installation and in translation tables. Microsoft Windows—Windows code page or character set corresponding to the meta variant. When installing Uniface on Windows, the Character set selection screen lists only the character sets supported by Windows (the first 11 character sets in the table). Unix—Unix character set corresponding to the meta variant. iSeries—Code Character Set Identifier (CCSID) corresponding to the meta variant. The iSeries systems are EBCDIC-based platforms. They are used only to run Uniface servers. Different character sets are used in different systems or different version of systems. Table 1. Installable Character Sets Type Meta Variant Microsoft Windows Unix Single-byte (LTR) Western European (or English) CP1252 ISO 8859-1 Central European CP1250 ISO 8859-2 Cyrillic CP1251 ISO 8859-5 Greek CP1253 ISO 8859-7 Single-byte (LTR+RTL) Arabic CP1256 CP708(7-bit) ISO 8859-6 ISO 9036(7-bit) Hebrew CP1255 ISO 8859-8 ISO 8859-8-1 Double-byte Simplified Chinese CP936 GB2312-80 Korean CP949 KSC5601-1992 Japanese CP932(Shift-JIS) ISO 2022-JP(EUC-JP) Traditional Chinese CP932 (BIG5) BIG5 More Information For more information on platform-specific character sets for, see the following: Developing International Software, second Edition, Microsoft programming series, Microsoft Press, 2002 ISO Standard IBM's iSeries Information Center Related Links Installable Character Sets Character sets that conform to one of several characters encoding systems can be installed into Uniface applications.
